headline: Is EQT’s $1.15 Billion Debt Tender and 2027 Note Redemption Altering The Investment Case For EQT (EQT)?
summary: In March 2026, EQT Corporation launched a cash tender offer of up to US$1.15 billion to repurchase multiple senior note issues, alongside plans to redeem all outstanding 6.500% Senior Notes due 2027, aiming to retire debt using cash on hand and, if needed, its revolving credit facility. This move to cut total debt and interest costs comes as analysts increasingly focus on EQT’s cash flow profile, highlighting how capital structure choices may influence the company’s long-term financial...

headline: JP Morgan Maintains Overweight on EQT, Raises Price Target to $72
summary: JP Morgan  analyst Arun Jayaram   maintains EQT (NYSE:EQT) with a Overweight and raises the price target from $68 to $72.

headline: Geopolitically Themed Investment Risks And Opportunities Take Center Stage
summary: Geopolitics and ever-changing macroeconomic forecasts have driven markets more than underlying fundamentals in the past six weeks. There has also been a lot of sector and industry rotation.
